steps.title: Put the equation in standard form
steps.content:
Move all terms to one side so the equation is written as a quadratic in $x$.

$$nx^2-18x=24x^2-9$$

$$(n-24)x^2-18x+9=0$$

steps.title: Use the discriminant condition
steps.content:
For two distinct real solutions, the discriminant must be positive.

$$(-18)^2-4(n-24)(9)>0$$

$$324-36(n-24)>0$$

$$324-36n+864>0$$

$$1188-36n>0$$

$$n<33$$

steps.title: Choose the greatest integer
steps.content: The value of $n$ must be an integer less than $33$. The greatest integer with that property is $32$. The answer is 32.
